originalPrompt:
Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS
Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None

subtasks.title: Initialize project repository
subtasks.description: Create the project directory, run npm init -y, and initialize a git repository with a README placeholder.
subtasks.successCriteria: A new project folder exists containing package.json and a .git directory; README.md placeholder is present.

subtasks.title: Install server and build dependencies
subtasks.description: Install Express and development tooling: tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, and optional dev tools like nodemon and concurrently.
subtasks.successCriteria: package.json lists express, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer and devDependencies installed; node_modules populated and no install errors.

subtasks.title: Configure Tailwind CSS build
subtasks.description: Initialize Tailwind and PostCSS configs, create a src/styles/input.css with @tailwind directives, and configure output directory for compiled CSS.
subtasks.successCriteria: tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js exist; an input CSS file with @tailwind directives is present; running the build produces a compiled CSS file in the public/dist folder.

subtasks.title: Create Express server to serve static assets and API
subtasks.description: Add server.js (or app.js) that serves the frontend static files and exposes a minimal JSON API (GET /api/color, POST /api/color) to read/update the current text color in memory.
subtasks.successCriteria: Server starts without errors and serves static files; GET /api/color returns a JSON color value and POST /api/color updates it (confirmed with curl or browser).

subtasks.title: Build frontend UI with Tailwind to display and change color
subtasks.description: Create index.html and a small app.js that render the 'hello world' text, provide a color picker and preset buttons, fetch initial color from the API, and POST updates when the user changes the color.
subtasks.successCriteria: The page displays 'hello world' and the UI lets a user change its color; color changes update the displayed text immediately and persist via the API (verified across reloads).

subtasks.title: Add npm scripts for build and development
subtasks.description: Add npm scripts: build:css, watch:css, start, and dev (concurrently runs server and tailwind watch) so the project can be run and developed easily.
subtasks.successCriteria: npm run build:css produces compiled CSS; npm run dev launches both the Express server and tailwind watcher without errors.

subtasks.title: Test app and write README
subtasks.description: Manually test functionality (UI, API, build scripts) and add a README with setup, run, and usage instructions plus example curl commands for the API.
subtasks.successCriteria: All listed features work end-to-end on a fresh clone following the README; README contains commands to install, run, and test the color change behavior.

decompositionStrategy: Top-down functional decomposition: first prepare environment and dependencies, then configure Tailwind, implement the Express backend, build the frontend UI to change text color, add convenience scripts, and finish with testing and documentation.
